name: Authentic Pizzelles
recipeInstructions:
Pizzelle, a cookie from Italy.
1. Place butter in a small saucepan and warm over low heat until melted. Remove from the heat and set aside until cooled.
2. Thoroughly stir together flour and baking powder in a bowl.
3. Beat eggs until frothy. Add sugar and lemon juice, continuing to beat until the mixture is light.
4. Beat in lemon zest and anise extract (or vanilla extract).
5. Add dry ingredients, beating lightly, until the mixture is smooth.
6. Lightly beat in cooled butter until incorporated.
7. Brush interior surfaces of a seasoned pizelle iron with flavorless vegetable oil, covering all grooves and indentations. Heat the iron until a drop of water sizzles when sprinkled on the interior surfaces.
8. Remove iron from heat and hold over waxed paper, dropping about 2 tablespoons batter onto the center of one side. Close the iron immediately and scrape off excess batter around the edges.
9. Place iron on burner and bake, turning from one side to the other every 20 seconds and opening the iron frequently to check the wafer for doneness.
10. When light gold on both sides, quickly remove pizzelles using tongs or a fork and lay flat on a wire rack until cool. Repeat process with remaining batter.
11. Store wafers in an airtight container for 3-4 days. Makes 14-15, 5-inch wafers.
